summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

Two inscribed angles intercept the same arc. What is true about them?

When two chords intersect inside a circle, the angle = ½(sum of intercepted arcs). Also, the products of their segments are equal: (segment₁)(segment₂) = (segment₃)(segment₄). The answer is 'They are congruent'. The other options — 'They are supplementary', 'They are complementary', and 'One is twice the other' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

If an intercepted arc measures 120°, what is the measure of the inscribed angle?

When two chords intersect inside a circle, the angle = ½(sum of intercepted arcs). Also, the products of their segments are equal: (segment₁)(segment₂) = (segment₃)(segment₄). The answer is '60°'. The other options — '120°', '240°', and '30°' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
